neighborhoods.name: Centro / Casco Antiguo (Alicante)
neighborhoods.description: Historic center beneath Santa Barbara Castle with the Explanada promenade, port area, and Central Market
neighborhoods.character: Urban walkable, Mediterranean port city, mix of historic and modern

housing.predominantType: Apartments in renovated historic buildings and modern blocks

neighborhoods.character_notes: Explanada de Espana is the social heart. Mercado Central for daily shopping. TRAM connects to northern Costa Blanca towns.

neighborhoods.name: Playa de San Juan
neighborhoods.description: Long sandy beach neighborhood north of Alicante center with a boardwalk promenade and resort atmosphere
neighborhoods.character: Beach lifestyle, modern, spacious, popular with both locals and expats

housing.predominantType: Modern apartments and beachfront condos, many with pools

neighborhoods.character_notes: 6km sandy beach. TRAM connects to Alicante center in 20 minutes. Quieter in winter. Good restaurants along the paseo.

neighborhoods.name: Javea (Xabia) - Port & Arenal
neighborhoods.description: Picturesque coastal town between Alicante and Valencia with a historic port area, sandy Arenal beach, and dramatic Montgo mountain backdrop
neighborhoods.character: Expat-popular coastal town, laid-back, scenic, strong British community

housing.predominantType: Villas with pools, townhouses, and apartments near Arenal beach

neighborhoods.character_notes: One of the largest British expat communities in Spain. Montgo Natural Park for hiking. Car recommended. Multiple international restaurants.

neighborhoods.name: Denia - Centre & Port
neighborhoods.description: Historic port town with a Moorish castle, working fishing port, and ferry connections to Ibiza and Mallorca
neighborhoods.character: Authentic Spanish town with growing expat community, gastronomic reputation

housing.predominantType: Apartments in town center and villas in surrounding urbanizations

neighborhoods.character_notes: UNESCO Creative City of Gastronomy. Ferry to Balearic Islands. More Spanish character than Javea. Quique Dacosta restaurant.

neighborhoods.name: Altea - Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Whitewashed hilltop village with blue-domed church, artist community, and sweeping Mediterranean views
neighborhoods.character: Artistic, bohemian, quiet, scenic hilltop with village charm

housing.predominantType: Townhouses in old village and apartments/villas in surrounding areas

neighborhoods.character_notes: Known as the 'artists' village' of Costa Blanca. TRAM connects to Alicante and Benidorm. Beautiful sunsets from the mirador.
